description: Question set for evaluating context-based question answering over Lattes CV data.

exact-match.description: Answer matches an accepted canonical answer after normalization.

answerability.description: Model correctly identifies that the answer is not available in the provided context.

main-topics.description: Identifies important topics from the researcher profile.

research-expertise.description: Infers expertise grounded in research activities, projects, or publications.

teaching-expertise.description: Infers expertise grounded in teaching, advising, or academic supervision.

main-fields.description: Identifies the main research fields of the researcher.

topic-coverage.description: Covers relevant themes expected for the task.

temporal-evolution.description: Describes change, progression, or expansion over time instead of listing isolated topics.

synthesis.description: Provides a synthesized answer rather than a raw list of items.

conciseness.description: Keeps the answer concise and proportional to the request.

clear-conclusion.description: States a clear conclusion or decision when the task requires one.

domain-fit.description: Connects the researcher profile to the target domain of the judgment task.

count.description: Provides the requested number of items.

item-structure.description: Each item follows the requested structure.

topic-relevance.description: Selected items are relevant to the requested topic.

justified-selection.description: The answer explains why the selected items are relevant.

uses-evidence.description: Uses evidence from the provided context to support the answer.

factual-correctness.description: Claims are factually correct with respect to the provided context.

grounded.description: Claims stay within the information supported by the provided context.

traceability.description: Claims can be mapped to specific evidence in the context.

context-dependence.description: The answer depends on the provided context rather than generic prior knowledge.

task-success.description: Overall fulfillment of the task intent.

questions.question: How many publications does the researcher have in 2026?
What skills or areas of expertise can be inferred from the researcher’s teaching and research activities?

judgingNotes: Look for expertise inferred from research topics, publications, or projects.
Do not reward generic software-related claims with no support in context.
Look for expertise supported by teaching, advising, or supervision activities.

criteria: major claims can be linked to activities, themes, publications, projects, or supervision
answer would not fit equally well for an arbitrary software engineering researcher

questions.question: How many children does the researcher have?

criteria: does not invent personal facts that are absent from context

questions.question: Summarize the researcher’s main fields of research in one concise paragraph based only on the provided information.

judgingNotes: Penalize overly long or repetitive answers.

questions.question: You are conducting research on microservices architecture. List 3 publications from this researcher that are most relevant to this topic. For each publication, include title, year, and a brief justification.

criteria: each recommended item can be matched to an available publication
selection reflects the researcher-specific candidate space

questions.question: Assess whether this researcher would be a suitable senior advisor for a research project on adaptive software architecture. Provide a clear conclusion and a brief justification based on the researcher’s experience and publications.

criteria: uses evidence from publications, projects, roles, teaching, or supervision
